title: Floral Velvety Syrian Phyllo Stir-fry
description: A solo-dinner Syrian dish featuring floral-flavored phyllo dough mixed with dumplings and served in a velvety sauce for a low-sodium-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Phyllo Dough
ingredients.notes: Use a mix of flavored and plain phyllo for added flavor.

ingredients.name: Ground Beef or Lamb
ingredients.notes: Opt for lean meat to keep the dish low in fat.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Low sodium soy sauce recommended for health-conscious choices.

ingredients.name: Water or Chicken Stock
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium stock if available for dietary preference.

ingredients.name: Fresh Herbs (optional)
ingredients.notes: Include fresh parsley, mint, or cilantro for additional flavoring.

instructions: Fold and lay out the phyllo sheets in a single layer on a baking sheet. Brush with oil and lightly sprinkle soy sauce over each sheet.
Arrange dumplings and ground meat evenly across the phyllo layers, ensuring no gaps between them for better binding.
Drizzle water or chicken stock over the top to ensure all ingredients are moistened, then cover loosely with aluminum foil and bake at 375°F for 10 minutes.
After baking, remove the foil and continue to bake uncovered until golden brown and crisp on top (approximately another 10-15 minutes).
Remove from oven, garnish with fresh herbs if desired, and serve hot.
